res00061.title: Introduction
res00061.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CONTENT id="_4046455_1"><TITLE value="Introduction"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><p><br><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-51963817_1" alt="" width="802" height="285" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;</p>
<p>Thanks for choosing North Metropolitan TAFE as your training provider.</p>
<p>You can use the menu on the left to navigate through this unit.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Learning Materials</strong> - In this<strong>&nbsp;</strong>section you will find all of the learning resources required for this unit.</li>
<li>You will find details of all of the assessments in the <strong>Assessment&nbsp;</strong>section</li>
<li><strong>My Grades </strong>is where you will find your assessment feedback once marked</li>
<li><strong>Blackboard Collaborate</strong> is an online Classroom where your lecturer may choose to meet or hold sessions</li>
<li>In the <strong>Communication</strong> section you will find tools to contact your lecturer or classmates</li>
<li>If you have any questions or problems, please see the <strong>Staff contacts&nbsp;</strong>section</li>
</ul>
<p><br></p>
<p>This unit describes the performance outcome, knowledge and skills required to recognise threats, risks and vulnerabilities to cyber security in an organisation.</p>
<p>The threats to an organisation include networks, machines, applications, data, users and infrastructure.</p>
<p>In your study, you will investigate common cyber security attack mechanisms and an introduction to threat management as well as security issues surrounding Internet of Things (IoT) devices.</p>
<p>You will also investigate the implementation of tools and systems an organisation can use for protection against cyber-attacks.</p></TEXT><TYPE
   value="H"/></BODY><DATES><CREATED value="2013-11-13 11:51:24 AWST"/><UPDATED value="2024-01-22 11:57:58 AWST"/><START

<CONTENT id="_3793144_1"><TITLE value="Session 4"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><p><br><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-46885301_1" alt="" width="695" height="269" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;</p><h5>News</h5><p><strong>1.<span style="font-size: 12pt;"> <a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2025/-undercooked---scam-reforms-rushed-through-parliament.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM25315" target="_blank" rel="noopener">‘Undercooked’: Scam reforms rushed through Parliament | Information Age | ACS&nbsp;</a></span></strong></p><p><strong><a href="https://www.patreon.com/posts/china-is-able-to-97117181?utm_medium=post_notification_email&amp;utm_campaign=patron_engagement&amp;utm_source=post_link" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span style="text-decoration: underline;">2.&nbsp; </span>&nbsp;<span style="font-size: 12pt;">ThreatWire 25 Jan 2024 | Patreon</span></a></strong> watch until end of 3rd story - 4 min total<br><em><strong>Threatwire</strong></em> a weekly video podcast which has a number of stories each week on hacking and other cyber security related events. Although USA based, it is still a good source of the nature of current threats and the failures that allowed them to succeed.<br>This weeks stories are</p><ol><li>USA Security and Exchange Commission (SEC) X-account hacked via a SIM swapping attack and because its 2FA was switched off</li><li>USA IT pen tester gets found guilty and fined for unauthorised penetration testing despite uncovering a very serious vulnerability of the target company</li><li>China uni develops a way of revealing the senders, recipients and contents of Airdrop (Apple to Apple wireless file transfer method)</li></ol><p>3. <a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2024/should-i-give-my-banking-password-to-a-mortgage-broker-.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM23027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Should I give my banking password to a mortgage broker? | Information Age | ACS</span></strong></a>&nbsp; - "Screen scraping" Vs Open Banking with Consumer Data Rights (CDR)</p><p>4. <span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2024/cyber-company-duped-by-north-korean-hacker.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM23027" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cyber company duped by North Korean hacker | Information Age | ACS</a> </strong><span style="font-size: 10pt;">- State sponsored threat actor tricked the usual vetting procedures when hired.</span></span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">5. <a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2025/china-based-backdoor-found-in-health-devices.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM25242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;">China-based backdoor found in health devices | Information Age | ACS</span></strong></a></span></span> - Medical IoT device&nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</p><h5><strong>Session Topic:</strong></h5><div>During the session we Investigate common and emerging cyber security attacks, and techniques.</div><div>Specifically we will be investigating:</div><div><ul style="list-style-type: disc;"><li style="list-style-type: inherit;">The known Cyber attacks identified and characterised</li><li style="list-style-type: inherit;">How they work, what vulnerabilities are exploited.</li></ul><p><strong><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-47094945_1" alt="" width="596" height="381" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"></strong></p><p>Then we will examine the Internet of Things (IoT);</p><ul style="list-style-type: disc;"><li style="list-style-type: inherit;">IoT devices are presented</li><li style="list-style-type: inherit;">The reasons for their deployment (why they are used)</li><li style="list-style-type: inherit;">The particular problems with ensuring their security, both standalone and on the network.</li></ul></div><ul style="list-style-type: disc;"></ul><h5><strong>Out of Session Activity:</strong></h5><ul><li>Review the PowerPoint presentations, and the notes provided by your lecturer.</li></ul><h5><strong>Portfolio Tasks:</strong></h5><p>&lt

res00100.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CONTENT id="_3793148_1"><TITLE value="Session 8"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><h4><br><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-46884693_1" alt="" width="489" height="340" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;</h4><h4>Introduction</h4><div>During this session we will be investigating the frameworks used to help organisations protect against cyber attacks.</div><p><br><br></p><h5>News</h5><ol><li>More bad news about the Cyber security professionals - looks like original report was from the UK, but reflects Australian concerns.<br><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/content/ia/article/2024/cyber-specialists-moonlighting-as-dark-web-criminals.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM20892" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cyber specialists moonlighting as dark web criminals | Information Age | ACS&nbsp;</a><br><br></span></strong></li><li>This next story is an update of the Microsoft Attack story from Session 3&nbsp;<br><a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/content/ia/article/2024/hackers-lurk-in-microsoft-systems-for-3-months.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM20892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Hackers lurk in Microsoft systems for 3 months | Information Age | ACS</strong></span></a>&nbsp;<br><br></li><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://www.abc.net.au/news/2025-03-20/notre-dame-uni-students-says-cyber-attack-playing-havoc/105075278" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Notre Dame uni students say outage creating enrolment, graduation, assignment mayhem - ABC News</a></strong></span>&nbsp;<br><br></li><li>&nbsp;(not News, but a look at verious Cyber Tools) <strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><a href="https://www.hirensbootcd.org/download/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Download | Hiren's BootCD PE</a></span></strong>&nbsp;</li></ol><div></div><p><br></p><h5>Topic</h5><div><div>This session you will e<span style="font-size: 13.3333px;">xamine methods used to protect an organisation’s data.</span></div><div><span style="font-size: 13.3333px;">The main focus will be on the following:</span></div><div><ul><li>MITRE adversarial tactics</li><li>Techniques and Common Knowledge (ATT&amp;CK)&nbsp;<br><br></li></ul></div></div><div>MITRE ATT&amp;CK (<strong>Adversarial Tactics, Techniques and Common Knowledge</strong>) is a framework, set of data matrices, and assessment tool developed by MITRE Corporation to help organizations understand their security readiness and uncover vulnerabilities in their defenses.&nbsp;<br><br></div><h5><strong>Out of Session Activity</strong></h5><div>Review the Lockheed Martin Cyber Kill Chain and the Mitre ATT&amp;CK framework.</div><div>Consider how they are related.</div><div>Importantly, the Cyber Kill Chain has a military origin with external threats.</div><p><br><br></p><h5>Portfolio Tasks</h5><div>Check assessments tab.</div><div>Complete outstanding work</div></TEXT><TYPE

<p><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-43740565_1" alt="" width="125" height="101" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"></p>
<p>After you finish the presentations please explore the Mitre ATT&amp;CK starting with the tour<br><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://attack.mitre.org/matrices/enterprise/?tour=true" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Matrix - Enterprise | MITRE ATT&amp;CK®</a></strong></span> &nbsp;<br><br>Don't just click <strong><em>next </em></strong>then <strong><em>next </em></strong>without spending a minute or so looking around each page to see what information it presents and its layout.<br>The tour ends at the versions page, but on that page there are plenty more links to explore.</p>
<p>Lastly, Known attackers have been organized into groups including their history of attacks and the techniques they use. <span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://attack.mitre.org/groups/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Groups | MITRE ATT&amp;CK®</a></strong></span>&nbsp;</p></TEXT><TYPE

res00101.title: Session 9
res00101.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CONTENT id="_3793149_1"><TITLE value="Session 9"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><h4><br><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-46884691_1" alt="" width="519" height="304" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;</h4>
<h4>Introduction</h4>
<div>During this session we will be examining various ways of protecting an organisation's data.</div>
<div>Typically the organisation's data is one of the most valuable assets owned by the organisation.</div>
<div>Protection includes preventing theft, unauthorised access, and unauthorised changes or corruption.</div>
<p><br></p>
<h5>News</h5>
<ol>
<li><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2024/why-can-t-i-get-a-graduate-job-in-cyber-security-.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM23519" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Why can’t I get a graduate job in cyber security? | Information Age | ACS</a>&nbsp;<br></span></strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Does this apply to you as a potential TAFE graduate?</span><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;"> DISCUSS with the lecturer!<br><br></span></strong></li>
<li><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><a href="https://australiancybersecuritymagazine.com.au/paying-cyber-ransoms-still-lands-organisations-in-hot-water/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Paying cyber-ransoms still lands organisations in hot water - Australian Cyber Security Magazine</a><br><br></span></strong></li>
<li><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><a href="https://australiancybersecuritymagazine.com.au/new-national-cyber-security-coordinator-announced/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">New National Cyber Security Coordinator announced - Australian Cyber Security Magazine</a> </span></strong></li>
</ol>
<div></div>
<h5 id="anonymous_element_24">Topic</h5>
<div>During this session we will continue to examine methods used to protect an organisation's data.</div>
<div>We will consider various policies, tools and systems for cyber protection.</div>
<div>These include</div>
<ul>
<li>Host Intrusion Detection Systems,</li>
<li>Cryptography, and</li>
<li>various means of obfuscating data</li>
</ul>
<div></div>
<h5><strong>Out of Session Activity</strong></h5>
<div>Review the various methods for protecting data in your own personal or organisational use.</div>
<div>Think about how these compare to what was discussed during this session.</div>
<div></div>
<h5>Portfolio Tasks</h5>
<div>Check assessments tab.</div>
<div>Complete outstanding work</div></TEXT><TYPE

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CONTENT id="_3793166_1"><TITLE value="Presentation"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><p>This is presentation for Session 9</p><p><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-43740565_1" alt="" width="125" height="101" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"></p><p style="font-size: 13.3333px;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://youtu.be/44d1z9bc5JA" target="_blank" rel="noopener">HIDS Vs NIDS</a></strong></span></p><p style="font-size: 13.3333px;"><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><a href="https://www.indusface.com/learning/deep-packet-inspection-dpi/?utm_campaign=newsletter-april-2025&amp;utm_medium=email&amp;_hsenc=p2ANqtz--HNPzZv7XCneWT7-DYJLFYfnM-rpS_RvLDt0CQqWMhdJkGeFnhKG_sHrZZCoCAhoZyowqSeGTgJ0agG5DQmf6tJtcOe7hQ6Dc9DYrX2-ETdyNgpEY&amp;_hsmi=356750153&amp;utm_source=email-newsletter" target="_blank" rel="noopener">What Is Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) and How It Works</a> </span></strong></p><p style="font-size: 13.3333px;"><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Cyber Security Policy<br><a href="https://business.gov.au/online-and-digital/cyber-security/create-a-cyber-security-policy" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Create a cyber security policy | business.gov.au</a></span></strong></p><p style="font-size: 13.3333px;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Cyber Security Procedures and Protocols</strong></span> include the following instruction steps:</p><ol><li style="list-style-type: decimal;" data-priority=""><strong>Identify the assets</strong>&nbsp;that need protection.</li><li style="list-style-type: decimal;" data-priority=""><strong>Protect the assets</strong>&nbsp;by implementing security measures.</li><li style="list-style-type: decimal;" data-priority=""><strong>Monitor the system</strong>&nbsp;for any security threats.</li><li style="list-style-type: decimal;" data-priority=""><strong>Respond to incidents</strong>&nbsp;promptly.</li><li style="list-style-type: decimal;" data-priority=""><strong>Recover</strong>&nbsp;from any cybersecurity breaches.</li></ol><p><strong>Note: Procedures</strong> are usually formally formatted as a series of numbered steps to be followed.<br><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Dynamic playbooks</strong></span> contain these procedures are are used to direct users, depending on their pre-defined roles how to response to specific incidences.<br>The "dynamic" part means there are a choice to make from the number of "next steps" options to take depending on the results of the previous step.&nbsp;</p><p style="font-size: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><h4><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-46884689_1" alt="" width="311" height="311" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;</h4><h4>Introduction</h4><div>During this session we will investigate the behaviour based approach to cyber security.</div><div>This is an attempt to out-think the adversary, and anticipate as much as possible.<br><br></div><div></div><pre><strong><span style="font-size: 14pt;">News</span></strong></pre><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>1. <a href="https://australiancybersecuritymagazine.com.au/industry-responds-to-protective-security-policy-framework-directive/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Industry Responds to Protective Security Policy Framework Directive - Australian Cyber Security Magazine</a></strong></span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">2. Just to show even the experts can be scammed, <strong><a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2025/have-i-been-pwned-creator-falls-victim-to-scam.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM25705" target="_blank" rel="noopener">'Have I Been Pwned' creator falls victim to scam | Information Age | ACS</a></strong>&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;">3. Failure of governance by Australian Super Funds, <strong><a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2025/superannuation-attack-an--industry-wake-up-call-.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM25758" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Superannuation attack an ‘industry wake-up call’ | Information Age | ACS</a>&nbsp;</strong></span></p><pre><strong><span style="font-size: 14pt;">Not Quite the News</span></strong></pre><div><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>From CISCO for your reading pleasure</strong></span></p><ol><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a alt="" href="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-54040084_1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Busting Passwordless Myths_CISCO Duo.pdf</a></strong></span></li><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a alt="" href="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-54040085_1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Zero Trust Frameworks - CISCO Duo.pdf</a></strong></span></li><li><strong style="font-size: 12pt;"><a alt="" href="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-54040086_1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Duo-Cisco-Secure-Multi-Factor-Authentication-Guide-2022.pdf</a></strong></li></ol><p>&nbsp;<br>&nbsp;</p><h4>&nbsp;Topic</h4></div><div>During this session we will continue to investigate and examine means to proect an organisation's data.</div><div>We will focus specifically on the following topics:</div><div><p>Three dimensions of security</p><ul><li>IT Security Management Framework - ITSM (New topic)</li><li>Behavioural Approaches</li><li>CIA Triad</li><li>States of Data</li></ul></div><div><br><br></div><h5><strong>Out of Session Activity</strong></h5><div>Review the various frameworks and approaches presented, including</div><ul><li>NIST</li><li>McCumber cube</li><li>ISO Cyber Security model</li></ul><div></div><p><br><br></p><h5>Portfolio Tasks</h5><div>Check assessments tab.</div><div>Complete outstanding work</div></TEXT><TYPE

res00123.title: Presentation
res00123.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CONTENT id="_3793160_1"><TITLE value="Presentation"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><p>This is the presentation for Session 10</p><p><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-43740565_1" alt="" width="125" height="101" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"></p><p>The process for an organization to obtaining a ISO27001:2022 compliance certification is set out in the following document<br><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a alt="" href="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-54250626_1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">27001 Cheat Sheet.pdf</a>&nbsp; Open and read please!<br></strong></span>Notes:</p><ol><li>There is a 2022 update to ISO27001 with organizations expected to comply with the updated standard by October 2025.</li><li>Apart from a successful checklist/audits/certification, the actual output of the ISO27001 process is a customised <span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Information Security Management System (ISMS)</strong> <span style="font-size: 10pt;">for that organization.<br></span></span>The ISMS consists of a complete set of cyber security policy and procedure documents.<br>These must be regularly reviewed and updated to keep ISO27001 compliance.<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;</li></ol></TEXT><TYPE
   value="H"/></BODY><DATES><CREATED value="2023-07-24 13:40:45 AWST"/><UPDATED value="2025-04-08 08:18:12 AWST"/><START

<CONTENT id="_3793173_1"><TITLE value="Classroom task"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><p>Form into 6 groups and present Powerpoints on the 2 new dimensions of<span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong> the McCumber Cube:</strong></span></p><p>3 x States of Data&nbsp; &nbsp;and 3 x Security Countermeasures&nbsp; &nbsp; <img alt="Image result for mccumber cube" src="https://d3i71xaburhd42.cloudfront.net/46bf9dd6df581bc0e75bc1a8e0228143042c33d4/2-Figure1-1.png" width="394" height="417"></p><p><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Privacy vs Confidentiality</strong></span>&nbsp; &nbsp;Discuss the differences from a legal standpoint&nbsp; &nbsp;(Cisco course 2.2.1.4)</p><p><img alt="Image result for privacy vs confidentiality" src="https://securitytoday.com/-/media/SEC/Security-Products/Images/2014/05/Mcafee_Privacy_Security_Confidential_McAfee_large2.jpg" width="437" height="310"></p><p><br></p><p>What is meant by the <span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>5 Nines</strong></span>?</p><p><img alt="Image result for 5 nines" src="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Gary_Weckman/publication/221056071/figure/tbl1/AS:669127739981829@1536543893512/MTTF-MTR-combinations-producing-five-nines.png" width="431" height="170"></p><p><br></p><p>With a partner complete Cisco <span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>Activity 2.2.3.4 and 2.4.3.5&nbsp;</strong></span> &nbsp;Share and discuss.</p><p>Read 2.5.1.2 ISO domains and as a class, complete<span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong> activity 2.5.1.5</strong></span></p><p>Investigate the following <span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>professional organisations</strong></span> and identify how they might assist you in your studies and/or future careers:</p><p>CERT&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;www.cert.org</p><p>SANS&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;www.sans.org</p><p>MITRE&nbsp; &nbsp; www.mitre.org</p><p>ISC2&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;www.isc2.org</p><p>NIST&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; www.nist.gov</p></TEXT><TYPE

<CONTENT id="_3793152_1"><TITLE value="Session 12"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><h4><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-46884685_1" alt="" width="632" height="355" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;</h4><h4>Introduction</h4><p>Cyber attacks are a widespread reality in today's world, and they are not going away soon.</p><p>Today we will review what we need to do about it.</p><p><br><br></p><h5>News</h5><ol><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2024/300-000-fraud-attempts-blocked-after-optus.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM21613" target="_blank" rel="noopener">300,000 fraud attempts blocked after Optus breach | Information Age | ACS</a></strong></span> <br>.</li><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://www.watoday.com.au/search?text=cyber+security" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Search | WAtoday</a> </strong>(cyber security search for West Australian newspaper)<strong><br>&nbsp; </strong></span></li></ol><div></div><div></div><h5 id="anonymous_element_24">Topic</h5><div><div>Cyber attacks and their consequences are very serious.</div><div>During this session we will investigate the nature of typical cyber attacks, and will focus on the following:</div><ul><li>Typical attacks</li><li>Best in class responses</li><li>Best in class preventions</li><li>An attack type that will only get worst -&nbsp;<br><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://youtu.be/CMHL1bPtQdI?si=Rd_7w-cQKlWlaSYc" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Zero-Click Attacks with AI amplification - IBM Technology</a></strong></span></li></ul></div><p><br></p><h5><strong>Out of Session Activity</strong></h5><div>Two things this week.</div><div>1. Review the presentation.</div><div>2. Have a look at the data breach at the property giant Meriton.</div><div>What should Meriton do now?</div><div></div><p><br></p><h5>Portfolio Tasks</h5><div>Check assessments tab.</div><div>Complete outstanding work</div></TEXT><TYPE

<CONTENT id="_3793153_1"><TITLE value="Session 13"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><h4><br><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-46884683_1" alt="" width="508" height="316" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;</h4><h4>Introduction</h4><div>During this session we will investigate the frameworks designed to help identify vulnerabilties and improve cyber defences.</div><h5>News</h5><ol><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=369FyDCjecg" target="_blank" rel="noopener">The BIGGEST Software Supply Chain Attack in History - Threat Wire</a><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3DxMHGRKJNs" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> </a>&nbsp; (4.5 min)</strong></span><br>.</li><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong><a href="https://www.aph.gov.au/Parliamentary_Business/Bills_Legislation/Bills_Search_Results/Result?bId=r7250" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cyber Security Bill 2024 – Parliament of Australia (aph.gov.au)</a> </strong></span></li></ol><div></div><h5>Topic</h5><div>Managing the cyber security of any sized organisation is generally a daunting prospect.</div><div>Each organisation has different requirements and presents different vulnerabilities - even when the organisations in question may have similar network infrastructures.</div><div>In order to deal with these challenges, various frameworks have been developed to assist the organisation's cyber defence personnel.</div><div>During this session we will i<span style="font-size: 13.3333px;">nvestigate the current Cyber Security Frameworks (CSF), focussing on:</span></div><div><ul><li>ISO 27000 series (see Session 8)</li><li>NIST</li><li>Essential Eight</li></ul></div><div><br><br></div><h5><strong>Out of Session Activity</strong></h5><div>Review the powerpoint, and the supplied video on NIST.</div><p><br></p><h5>Portfolio Tasks</h5><div>Check assessments tab.</div><div>Complete outstanding work</div></TEXT><TYPE

   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><h4><br><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-56620587_1" alt="" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;<br><img src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-56620545_1" alt="" height="576" width="740" style="border: 0px solid #000000;"><br>&nbsp;<br>&nbsp;</h4><h4>Introduction</h4><div>The CIS (Center for Internet Security) Critical Security Controls are a prioritized set of actions for cybersecurity.</div><div>Thes controls form a defense-in-depth set of specific and actionable best practices to manage and deal with the most common cyber attacks</div><div>During this session we will examine the CIS controls&nbsp;</div><p><br><br></p><h5>News</h5><ol><li>More Aussi Telcos in trouble, <a href="https://ia.acs.org.au/article/2025/dodo--iprimus-data-breach-sees-email-and-sim-cards-hacked.html?ref=newsletter&amp;deliveryName=DM28782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><strong><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Dodo, iPrimus data breach sees email and SIM cards hacked | Information Age | ACS&nbsp;</span></strong></a><br><br></li><li><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><span res00136.title: Assessment Overview
res00136.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CONTENT id="_3549778_1"><TITLE value="Assessment Overview"/><TITLECOLOR value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><p><span style="font-size: small;">All assessments for the unit or cluster can be accessed here and are required to be completed by the due dates as stated in the Learning and Assessment Plan.</span></p> 
<p><span style="font-size: small;"><strong>Submission Warning</strong></span></p> 
<div> 
 <div>
  <span style="font-size: small;">Once you have completed your assessment and wish to submit it you MUST be aware that when you click the " <strong>Submit</strong>" button you are formally declaring that the <strong>attached work is your own</strong>. Do not click " <strong>Submit</strong>" if this is not the case, and do not allow others to access your Blackboard account as you will be held accountable for anything submitted using your login details.</span>
 </div> 
 <div></div> 
 <div>
  <br> 
  <div>
   <span style="font-size: small;"><strong>When completing any submission you are acknowledging the following:</strong></span>
  </div> 
  <div></div> 
  <br> 
  <div> 
   <div style="margin-left: 30px;">
    <span style="font-size: small;">a. The attached submission is completely my own work </span> 
    <br> 
    <span style="font-size: small;"> b. I have correctly cited all sources of information used in this work (if required) </span> 
    <br> 
    <span style="font-size: small;"> c. I have kept a copy of this assessment (where practicable) </span> 
    <br> 
    <span style="font-size: small;"> d. I understand a copy of my assessment will be kept by the NMTAFE for their records </span> 
    <br> 
    <span style="font-size: small;"> e. I understand my assessment may be selected for use in the NMTAFE’s validation and audit process to ensure student assessment meets requirements</span>
   </div> 
   <div>
    <br> 
    <span style="font-size: small;"> <strong>Results</strong></span>
   </div> 
   <div></div> 
   <div> 
    <div> 
     <div>
      <span style="font-size: small;">Results for assessments in this unit/cluster can be viewed by going to&nbsp;My Grades in the left hand menu. If you have any queries regarding your assessment results, comments and suggestions, please contact your lecturer. If we do not hear from you, we will assume your&nbsp;acknowledgement&nbsp;and acceptance of the feedback.</span>
     </div> 
    </div> 
   </div> 
  </div> 
 </div> 
</div></TEXT><TYPE

res00146.title: Submission and Documentation Instructions
res00146.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CONTENT id="_3793111_1"><TITLE value="Submission and Documentation Instructions"/><TITLECOLOR
  value="#000000"/><DESCRIPTION
   value=""/><BODY><TEXT><div>You are required to submit all of your documented evidence for the below work,&nbsp;&nbsp;<strong><em>each of the submissions must contain the following or they will be rejected</em></strong></div>
<ol style="list-style-type: decimal;">
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;"><strong>Activities</strong>:
<ol style="list-style-type: decimal;">
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Evidence that you completed each part of the activity, these should be signed screen shots (one option would be the snip tool in Windows)</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;"><strong>Packet Tracer Activities</strong>:
<ol style="list-style-type: decimal;">
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">The completed Packet Tracer file</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">The completed documentation that accompanies the Packet Tracer file including (open the PDF files in Microsoft Word to edit):
<ol style="list-style-type: decimal;">
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Screen shots of you completing the tasks</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Answers to any questions in the document</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Any other evidence that you feel will help you prove you completed the work</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;"><strong>Lab Activities</strong>:
<ol style="list-style-type: decimal;">
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">A .txt file for each config of each device (for example if your exercise has 2 routers and a switch you should include 3 files called R1.txt, R2.txt and S1.txt with the copy and pasted running-configuration of the devices in each)</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Completed lab documentation including:<br>
<ol style="list-style-type: decimal;">
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Screen shots to demonstrate you completed steps in the labs</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Answers to any questions in the document</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Any other evidence you feel will help you prove you completed the work</li>
</ol>
</li>
</ol>
<div><strong>All documentation</strong>:</div>
<ol style="list-style-type: decimal;">
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Screen shots of you entering every command required by the document (for example: if the document asks you to configure a router network interface, you must show screen shots to verify you did).</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Screen shots to verify your commands&nbsp;are correctly configured. This means if you are configuring networks or routing you must&nbsp;<strong><em>demonstrate&nbsp;</em></strong>that they work, this should be done through testing (ping,&nbsp;debug&nbsp;or&nbsp;show&nbsp;commands will be useful here (most labs will ask you to verify instructions anyway)).</li>
<li style="list-style-type: inherit;">Where appropriate, sign your screenshots, or add a "whoami" insert. Example here:<br><img style="height: auto;" src="@X@EmbeddedFile.requestUrlStub@X@bbcswebdav/xid-30139864_1" alt="" width="223" height="131">&nbsp;<span bb:beforeshowfunc="" bb:overwritenavitems="" bb:menuorder="openId_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,open_with_browser_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,open_with_bbdrive_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,openWebId_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,view360Id_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,*separator*,*separator*,ax-ally-placeholder_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,ax-ally-markForAxAnalysis_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,*separator*,*separator*,content_settings_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,content_editSettings_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,content_edit_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,properties_folder_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,itemcarot_managerecord_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,comments_tool_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,subscriptions_list_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,content_readfile_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,content_overwrite_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,passes_tool_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,permissions_tool_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,content_add_alignment_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b896407,content_tracking_06ed26c2fb8c4962980422777b89640
